steps.title: Add the equations to eliminate 8x
steps.content:
The coefficients of $8x$ are $+5$ and $-5$, which are opposites. Adding the equations eliminates this variable:
$$[5(8x) + 4(6y)] + [-5(8x) + 4(6y)] = 12 + 12$$
$$8(6y) = 24$$
$$6y = 3$$

steps.title: Substitute and calculate 8x + 6y
steps.content:
Substitute $6y = 3$ into the first equation to find $8x$:
$$5(8x) + 4(3) = 12$$
$$5(8x) = 0$$
$$8x = 0$$
Now compute the sum:
$$8x + 6y = 0 + 3 = 3$$
That matches choice C — C is correct.
